answer:
For Iqbal, 'dawat' (call/invitation/prophetic mission) is the Muslim community's universal, ongoing duty to bear witness to God's Oneness and to spread the message of monotheism to all of humanity. It is not a completed historical achievement but an unfulfilled vocation that defines the Muslim's purpose in the world.

Several dimensions emerge from the corpus:

1. Dawat as the founding act: In Shikwa (Bang-e-Dara Part 3, ch. 20), Iqbal reminds God that it was the Muslims who first carried the prophetic call — 'Whose call to prayer awakened all Your world, both false and true?' and 'We spoke Your sacred Word beneath the sword's descending line' and 'We etched the Creed of One upon the hearts of all mankind.' Before Islam, no one proclaimed God's name; the Muslims took this responsibility upon themselves.

2. Dawat as unfinished mission: In the long poem 'Dil se jo baat nikalti hai' (Bang-e-Dara Part 3, ch. 4), Iqbal issues God's rebuke to the Muslims that their mission remains incomplete: 'From nations' eyes your truth is still concealed; The world has need of what you've not revealed. Your inner fire keeps this age alive; Your destiny is how the worlds will thrive. This is no time for rest, for work remains, The light of Oneness still must break its chains.' Dawat is thus the telos of Muslim existence — a cosmic responsibility not yet fulfilled.

3. Dawat as bearing witness: In Wataniyat (Bang-e-Dara Part 3, ch. 25), Iqbal ties dawat to the Prophetic example: 'bear you witness to the Prophet's truth today.' In Iqbal's view, this witness is not confined to one homeland — the Muslim must transcend territorial nationalism to carry the message everywhere.

4. Dawat as the universal prayer-call: In Tarana-e-Milli (Bang-e-Dara Part 3, ch. 23), Iqbal celebrates 'Through Western valleys did our prayer-call sound' — the adhan as the embodiment of dawat, a call that knows no borders.

In sum, for Iqbal, dawat is the defining mission of the Muslim nation (Ummah): to proclaim God's Oneness to a world still veiled from this truth, to liberate humanity from false gods (including modern idols like nationalism), and to complete this prophetic relay. The Muslim's very existence is justified by this mission; abandoning it amounts to self-annihilation as a community.

tool_calls.result_summary: Dil se jo baat nikalti hai: God's response — the Muslim's mission to complete the light of Oneness is unfinished; 'The world has need of what you've not revealed'

tool_calls.result_summary: Wataniyat: nationalism condemned as new idol; Muslims told to 'bear you witness to the Prophet's truth today'

tool_calls.result_summary: Tarana-e-Milli: 'Through Western valleys did our prayer-call sound' — dawat as universal adhan
